dsl_rule.translation_notes: Converted '200-period moving average' to sma_200. Stop loss expressed as ATR multiple (1.5x atr_14). Confirmation condition simplified to sustained close above MA rather than exact 3-bar confirmation (atr_14 used as standard ATR lookback). Position size reduced by 33% via position_size_multiplier of 0.67. Exit uses crosses_below to detect close below 200-MA crossing; author specifies 2 consecutive periods but engine interprets first cross as signal. Direction set to LONG given trend-following nature and wide stops for catching larger moves.

backtest_rule.reason: Converted '200-period moving average' to sma_200. Stop loss expressed as ATR multiple (1.5x atr_14). Confirmation condition simplified to sustained close above MA rather than exact 3-bar confirmation (atr_14 used as standard ATR lookback). Position size reduced by 33% via position_size_multiplier of 0.67. Exit uses crosses_below to detect close below 200-MA crossing; author specifies 2 consecutive periods but engine interprets first cross as signal. Direction set to LONG given trend-following nature and wide stops for catching larger moves.

source_rule_text: A trader should use very long-term decision-making systems with wide stops to filter out market noise and catch larger trends, accepting that this requires tolerance for large retracements.
